EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A slow-moving storm system brought areas of rain and snow to northern Arizona |March 6 in the afternoon through the morning of March 8th. The storm had three phases: most of the precipitation was with the first phase late on the 6th to early on the 7th...but snow levels were fairly high. Precipitation along the frontal band during the afternoon of March 7th lowered snow levels to near 5500 feet from Flagstaff westward. The precipitation ended during the morning of March 8th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brief but intense snow storm caused dangerous driving conditions on I-17 from Flagstaff to just south of Munds Park.  Arizona Department of Public Safety reported 40 motorist assist and slide-offs, 12 non-injury collisions, and two minor injury accidents. By the morning of March 8th,  Bellemont received 7.9 inches of snow,  Flagstaff Airport 7.3 inches of snow, Parks 6 inches,  Kachina Village 5.8 inches, Mountainaire 5.5 inches, and Munds Park 4 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong Pacific winter storm produced moderate valley rain and mountain snow to much of southeast Arizona. Heavy snow combined with strong winds to produce significant blowing and drifting at the higher elevations. Strong gusty winds also affected many valley locations during the evening hours of the 19th and the early morning hours of the 20th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: ASOS at Fort Huachuca measured a wind of gust of 69 mph at 11:15 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A fast moving storm moved across northern Arizona January 18 and 19th causing traffic problems in the Flagstaff area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow across the greater Flagstaff area cause numerous traffic problems.   Highway Patrol responded to over 150 requests for help, 14 non-injury collisions, and four injury collisions.   The Flagstaff Police department responded to 50 slide-offs and 21 requests for help. There were 16 non injury collisions and 2 injury collisions in the city limits.  There was one fatal crash about 6 miles east of Flagstaff.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong winter storm moved through Southeast Arizona on December 29th into December 30th. This system started relatively warm on the 29th with the first wave of precipitation and associated cold front with only some of the mountaintops seeing snow. However, an arctic cold front associated with this storm moved through December 30th and lowered snow levels to about 2500 feet with some areas as low as 3000 feet seeing accumulating snow. Some of the mountainous terrain across Southeast Arizona had about 2 feet of snow total.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Some parts of the Tucson Metro area including the Vail area had about 2 inches of snowfall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of storms fueled by a tropical moisture tap pounded the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin for several days.  Extremely heavy snow and widespread flooding resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Beaver Dam Wash flooded the community of Beaver Dam.  Six homes were washed away, 12 more were damaged, two mobile homes and a garage were destroyed, and a 300-400 foot stretch of Clark Gable Drive was washed out.  The water flowed into the Virgin River, which then flooded Littlefield, damaging a sewer lift station.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain, 0.93 inches in 29 minutes, hit western portions of Prescott.  Roads were covered with water and the creeks were running full.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain in Prescott.  A spotter reported 1.01 inches of rain in 45 minutes.  Another spotter reported 0.90 inches of rain in just 20 minutes.  Small streams overflowed their banks.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter reported 1.65 inches of rain in one hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain and flash flooding over and below the Schultz Fire burn area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain over the Schultz Fire burn caused more flash flooding.  Washes were running full and over topping roads.  Several local roads were closed.  Highway 89 was closed for 20 minutes due to the flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms hit the Holbrook area with heavy rain.  A funnel clouds was also sighted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was sighted about 10 miles east of Holbrook.  The funnel descended and ascended twice.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: One inch of rain fell at Green Base Lookout Tower in 20 minutes.  The storm total was 1.10 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An extremely moist southeast flow across Arizona brought widespread showers, thunderstorms,  heavy rain, and flash flooding to northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain produced flash flooding in Sycamore Canyon.  Four people became stranded near a swimming hole and had to be rescued.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The 89 Mesa Fire started on a Mesa about a mile west of Highway 89 about 12 miles north of Flagstaff on May 6,  2010.  This fire was human caused.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The 89 Mesa Mesa fire was touch off by workers welding.  Strong winds in the area fanned the flames and forced the clouser of Highway 89 for several hours on the afternoon of May 6th.  The fire burned a total of 523 acres including 46 acres on the east side of Highway 89.  Around 200 people fought the fire.  The estimated cost of fighting the fire was $330,000.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large storm system moving over the western United States and a strong cold front moving across northern Arizona brought high winds and blowing dust to much of the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility dropped to less than 100 feet at times in blowing dust.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced 3/4 inch diameter hail and heavy rain.  There was minor street flooding.  No damage was reported.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced hail from 1/2 to 1 inch in diameter.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flooding over Indian Route 12 near Cibecue.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two men were injured due to a lightning strike on a golf course.  One man suffered life threatening injuries, which he later died from.  Another man was about 50 feet away, and received a milder shock from the lightning strike.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported U.S. Highway 191 flooded near Wards Canyon and Clifton.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere resulted in numerous thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona on July 27th.  Storms initiated relatively early during the midday hours, with the primary threats being heavy flooding rain and damaging winds during the afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds downed several power poles and power lines near the intersection of Pima Mine Road and Nogales Highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere and slow storm motion led to thunderstorms which produced flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere and slow storm motion led to thunderstorms which produced flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere and slow storm motion led to thunderstorms which produced flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding started at about 1035 PM in Cienega Creek southeast of Tucson. Thereafter, heavy rains in the Tucson metro area led to flash flooding in numerous streets in Tucson including at least one swift water rescue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained anchored across northern Arizona on July 15th, with deep easterly flow across southeast Arizona. A disturbance rotating around the upper high in this easterly flow enhanced storm organization and intensity across central and western Pima county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds snapped three power poles in half near Catalina Highway and Melpomene Way.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to northern Arizona. The strong winds created blowing dust which closed roads again.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The dust reduced the visibility enough that Highway Patrol started escorting vehicles on I-40 from Two Guns to near Winslow by 900AM.  The interstate was closed from 1230 PM to 530 PM due to restricted visibility in blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred at Pipe Spring National Monument.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the afternoon of October 4th. Some of the storms produced strong and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter near Sacaton reported a thunderstorm wind gust estimated at 52 knots. No damage was reported with this strong wind.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system off the west coast of the United States and subtropical moisture left over from a tropical system (Karl) combined to produce heavy rain over portions of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across Gila County.  A spotter a mile east of Payson reported 1.20 inches of rain overnight.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the afternoon of October 4th. Some of the storms produced strong and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter at 7th Avenue and Camelback Road reported that strong gusty thunderstorm winds damaged a plastic patio roof and blew down a 2 inch diameter tree branch. The spotter estimated winds to be in excess of 40 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the afternoon of October 4th. Some of the storms produced strong and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in the town of Florence reported that a large Palo Verde tree was blown down by a strong thunderstorm wind gust, estimated to be at least 50 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Fast moving strong thunderstorms during the early morning hours on October 6th produced large hail over parts of south central Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spottter at Val Vista and University in Mesa reported nickel sized hail during the early morning hours of October 6th. Heavy rainfall was also occurring at the time of the large hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms over southeast Arizona produced gusty winds in excess of 40 mph. The gusty outflow winds spread to the northwest and produced a dust storm over the central deserts, including the Greater Phoenix area, during the late afternoon hours on October 15th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Radio operator N0ZFN in Kings Ranch reported dense blowing dust, with visibility reduced to 1/8th of a mile. Winds were estimated in excess of 40 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms over southeast Arizona produced gusty winds in excess of 40 mph. The gusty outflow winds spread to the northwest and produced a dust storm over the central deserts, including the Greater Phoenix area, during the late afternoon hours on October 15th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter in Sun Lakes reported dense blowing dust, with visibility reported to be less than one quarter of a mile. Winds were estimated to be in excess of 40 mph. Other trained weather spotters in the southeastern Phoenix metropolitan area, in communities such as Gilbert, Higley and Chandler, reported a dust storm with visibilities at or below one quarter of a mile. The dust storm caused significant disruption to traffic at the tail end of the rush hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms with extremely heavy rain led to mutiple collisions on Interstate 10 in downtown Phoenix during the afternoon hours on August 28. A total of 69 vehicles were involved in a series of crashes; one collision alone involved 35 vehicles and spanned over 2 miles from 16th street to 7th street. Trained spotters reported visibilities below one quarter mile in heavy rain in downtown Phoenix, and it was low visibility in heavy rain that led to the collisions. Seven people were taken to area hospitals and two were in serious condition. Fire officials medically evaluated at least 40 people.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong thunderstorms produced damaging microburst winds over portions of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix area, on August 24th. Strong winds caused damage to buildings, trees, and power poles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Yuma Daily Sun reported that motorists on Interstate 8 had hail damage to windows. The size and time of the apparent microburst were estimated. One woman had a minor injury due to the glass that blew in.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Although not as hot as July, the month of August was slightly hotter than the long term averages.  The high temperature in Phoenix on the 5th reached 112 degrees, just two degrees shy of the daily record.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Maricopa County reported 2 heat related deaths and 3 heat caused deaths in August. The hottest high temperature was on the 5th when it reached 112 degrees in Phoenix. However, the highest daily average temperature was 101 degrees on the 24th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the afternoon of October 4th. Some of the storms produced strong and damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter 3 miles south of Tonopah reported a wind gust estimated at 60 mph. The strong wind caused areas of low visibility in blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused washes to run in and around Woodruff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in and around Woodruff caused area washed to run high.  The main road through Woodruff was closed due to running water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure system off the west coast of the United States and subtropical moisture left over from a tropical system (Karl) combined to produce heavy rain over portions of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across central Navajo Country.  Rainfall amounts include: 1.75 inches (15 miles south of Holbrook), 1.50 inches (north of Joseph City), and 0.93 inches (north of Snowflake).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong thunderstorms developed across south-central Arizona during the afternoon of the 21st, as significant subtropical moisture flowed into the area from the south. The storms generated damaging microburst winds as well as very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported significant wind damage to portions of Stanfield. Several mobile homes were rolled over or destroyed. One house lost it's roof. Additional damage was reported in the vicinity of Javelina and Conejo Avenue. The damage was due to severe microburst winds estimated to be in excess of 80 knots.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong thunderstorms developed across south-central Arizona during the afternoon of the 21st, as significant subtropical moisture flowed into the area from the south. The storms generated damaging microburst winds as well as very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced 1.50 inches of rainfall in one hour, as measured by the gage at the Gila Bend Landfill. The rain fell from 1633MST to 1733MST. The heavy rainfall prompted the issuance of an urban and small stream flood advisory for the Gila Bend area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong thunderstorms developed across south-central Arizona during the afternoon of the 21st, as significant subtropical moisture flowed into the area from the south. The storms generated damaging microburst winds as well as very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm wind gust to 62 knots was measured at the Gila Bend Landfill weather station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered strong thunderstorms developed across south-central Arizona during the afternoon of the 21st, as significant subtropical moisture flowed into the area from the south. The storms generated damaging microburst winds as well as very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter near Gila Bend measured a total of 0.90 inches of rainfall in a 30 minute period. The heavy rain prompted issuance of an urban and small stream flood advisory for the Gila Bend area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trained spotters reported that throughout Maricopa, over 13 power poles were blown down by thunderstorm microburst wind estimated to be in excess of 60 kt. In addition, numerous buildings sustained roof damage and trees up to 10 inches in diameter were blown over.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported that power poles were blown down at 19th Avenue and Dunlap. The poles were downed by thunderstorm microburst winds estimated to be in excess of 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter near South Mountain Park reported that multiple large tree limbs, at least 7 inches in diameter, were blown down by strong thunderstorm winds estimated to be in excess of 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Phoenix measured 1.24 inches of rainfall between 1800MST and 1845MST. The rain fell in the area near 16th Street and Northern.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced locally heavy rainfall across portions of west central Pinal county, including the town of Maricopa and the Ak-Chin Village. Radar and trained spotters indicated up to 3 inches of rain fell along the Santa Rosa and Green washes, and several reports of curb-to-curb street flooding in Maricopa were received.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power poles were blown down at 19th Ave and Dunlap.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms, some with heavy rain, gusty winds and blowing dust, affected portions of south central Arizona on July 24.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced locally heavy rainfall, with rainfall totals in excess of one inch. Heavy rain led to flooding of washes in the areas near the intersection of Highway 60 and Highway 188.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the evening of July 27, and they produced locally heavy rainfall. Rainfall totals exceeded one inch in some locations, including Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain produced 1.46 of rainfall within one hour in Apache Junction, in the area near Superstition Drive and Ironwood. The heavy rain caused washes to run and led to local street flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
Very hot conditions along with sufficient humidity resulted in strong to severe thunderstorms in parts of Southern Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall led to very low visibilities, as well as local flooding. Pea sized hail was also reported in Globe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over portions of south central Arizona on the evening of July 27, and they produced locally heavy rainfall. Rainfall totals exceeded one inch in some locations, including Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with heavy rain dropped one half inch of rainfall within 30 minutes between 1800MST and 1830MST, as measured by a trained spotter 10 miles southeast of Apache Junction. Rainfall was sufficient to cause flooding of normally dry washes in the area, as well as local street flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hail and gusty winds fell across remote areas generally east of GLobe Monday afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large thunderstorm complex resulted in heavy rain and 1/4 inch hail just east of Globe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving strong thunderstorms hit parts of Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Washes and low-lying areas became flooded after 0.70 inches fell in about an hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Brief strong winds produced near zero visibility followed by heavy rains and local flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility near zero reported south of Gilbert and Riggs in southern Chandler.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy rains were reported by spotters.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy downpours from slow moving thunderstorms resulted in .80 inch in about an hour.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain produced standing water across Interstate 10.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ten to eleven inches of water flowed down roadways in Golden Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across south central Arizona, in the Wickenburg area as well as portions of southern Gila county, on July 23. The storms produced strong gusty winds, and locally heavy rain which led to flooding of roads and washes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very strong thunderstorm winds, gusting to 60 mph, downed a number of branches. Locally heavy rain was reported as well, with one half of an inch reported in just 30 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed across south central Arizona, in the Wickenburg area as well as portions of southern Gila county, on July 23. The storms produced strong gusty winds, and locally heavy rain which led to flooding of roads and washes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall from strong thunderstorms produced areas of flooding in the Wickenburg area. Standing and running water was reported near the intersection of highways 93 and 60, not far from the center of Wickenburg. Rainfall rates up to three quarters of an inch per hour were also reported in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled isolated thunderstorms over Mohave County. One storm produced hail and flooding.
Monsoon moisture fueled isolated thunderstorms over Mohave County. One storm produced hail and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rocks and debris were washed across the road to Hualapai Mountain Park in three areas.  A nearby rain gage recorded 2.13 inches of rain in 30 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled more thunderstorms over Mohave County. One storm produced brief flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water briefly covered U.S. Highway 93 near Wikieup.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down power lines and blew awnings off several mobile homes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  A few storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water up to two and a half feet deep flowed across roads, washed out a driveway, and also took out several signs and shrubs.  A rain gage nearby measured 2.76 inches of rain in one hour.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  A few storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A firefighter and a motel employee nearby both estimated 60 mph winds.  A roof was torn off a mobile home, several power lines were blown down, and a Joshua tree was blown down.  Hail up to dime size also fell.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled scattered thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert.  A few storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Over two feet of water flowed across Pierce Ferry Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding closed several intersections in Bullhead City and washed debris onto roads as well.  Five people were rescued from swift water on Riverview Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled several rounds of thunderstorms over the Mojave Desert, with the greatest coverage of storms on the 26th and 27th.  Several storms produced severe weather and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds estimated by ADOT at 60-70 mph blew down a shed, power lines, and several trees (which closed U.S. Highway 93 for a time).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure center over California continued to push abundant moisture over Arizona producing widespread showers and thunderstorms.  A jet maximum rotating around the low and into western Arizona helped produce large-scale lift and enhanced up-slope across the Mogollon Rim. The vertical wind shear also provided an environment for enhanced thunderstorm development.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized (1 inch in diameter) fell in Bagdad.  Minor street flooding was also reported.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large hail fell near Payson. The majority of the hail was 1.25 inches in diameter with the largest stones were 2.25 inches in diameter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain in the Tsegi area caused flash flooding over Highway 160.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement officials reported flash flooding over Highway 160.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Antelope Canyon during the night.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The tour company at Antelope Canyon reported flash flooding in the canyon after midnight.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding in House Rock Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Bureau of Land Management reported flash flooding along House Rock Road--the road was river of water.  Coyote Wash flooded over House Rock Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding in House Rock Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny sized hail was reported in the Buckskin Gulch and Coyote Buttes areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding in Havasu Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A river gage on Havasu Creek showed a three foot rise.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain and flash flooding in the Bagdad area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding across Highway 97 at Skunk Creek (MP 158) and Boulder Creek (MP 163).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain that lead to flash flooding in the Salina area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain and flooding made Route 4 impassable due to high water between mile marker 40 and 43.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused heavy rain and flash flooding in the Pinon area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Wepo Wash flooded near Pinon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain and flash flooding in the Bagdad area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer in Bagdad reported 1.45 inches of rain in 45 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced damaging winds and small hail over central Apache Country.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damaging thunderstorm winds hit the Petrified Forest National Park.  Stop signs were blown down and small trees were snapped.  A wind sensor measured a peak wind gust of 107 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell in Concho and caused some culvert damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain (2.60 inches in 3 hours) caused some damage to culverts in the area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a land spout type tornado about 15 miles north of Chinle.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Navajo Police Department observed a funnel cloud west of Many Farms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flooding, washed out roads, and washed out bridges in the Wheatfields area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in and around the Wheatfields area.  Roads were washed out in several areas and a bridge or two was washed out.  Portions of Canyon de Chelly were evacuated as a precaution.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Snowbowl Road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced 2.20 inches of rain at Hart Prairie Lodge at Arizona Snowbowl. Small hail accumulated to a dept of one inch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced heavy rain and flash flooding over Snowbowl Road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water flowed over Snowbowl Road near the ski lodge.  No significant damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An exceptionally strong Pacific storm system impacted southeast Arizona, producing a wide variety of weather.  Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times.  Also, several severe thunderstorms produced wind damage during the evening of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The telescopes on Mt. Lemmon at 9100 feet had at least 12 inches of snow. This combined with strong wind resulted in low visibility and blowing snow producing blizzard conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture combined with a weak upper level disturbance to generate thunderstorms which produced very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: San Simon Wash west of Pisinimo overflowed its banks, cresting at 8.3 feet, just above its 7 foot flood stage. A tributary, Gu Vo Wash, flooded Indian Road 28.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture combined with a weak upper level disturbance to generate thunderstorms which produced very heavy rainfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain in Nogales, Sonora caused the Nogales Wash to rise 7 feet in less than one hour. The wash rose above flood stage, causing erosion to one roadway in Nogales, Arizona.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant moisture and weak flow aloft led to thunderstorms producing very heavy rainfall and isolated microbursts.
A backdoor cold front acted on a warm and moist air mass to produce severe thunderstorms.  Wet microbursts and flash flooding were the results.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a palm tree at the Randolph Community Center. A fire was ignited, which spread to surrounding brush, prompting the evacuation of the center.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A backdoor cold front acted on a warm and moist air mass to produce severe thunderstorms.  Wet microbursts and flash flooding were the results.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local Law Enforcement reported several trees and power lines down after thunderstorms moved through Whetstone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A backdoor cold front acted on a warm and moist air mass to produce severe thunderstorms.  Wet microbursts and flash flooding were the results.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: After a majority of the thunderstorm activity had started to wind down, lightning struck a house in Oro Valley.  The house then caught fire causing extensive damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A backdoor cold front acted on a warm and moist air mass to produce severe thunderstorms.  Wet microbursts and flash flooding were the results.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms redeveloped over central Tucson, moved westward and generated high winds causing a car port to collapse in midtown Tucson and a 63 mph wind gust to be recorded at Ryan Air Field.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An exceptionally strong Pacific storm system impacted southeast Arizona, producing a wide variety of weather.  Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times.  Also, several severe thunderstorms produced wind damage during the evening of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported near zero visibility in blowing dust at mile marker 210 on Interstate 10.

Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported near zero visibility due to blowing dust in Avra Valley, along Pump Station Road.  The air traffic control tower at Davis-Monthan Air Force Base reported 1/4 mile visibility in blowing dust.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sixteen hikers were trapped on Sabino Canyon Trail at approximately 11 AM on January 21st after the stream rose above its banks, covering low water crossings.  The San Simon and Vamori Washes in the Tohono Oodham Nation rose 1-2 feet out of their banks during the evening of January 21st.  Several other washes flowed out of their banks, resulting in barricaded roadways near Saguaro National Park East and West, including East Tucson and Avra Valley. A motorist was trapped in the Canada del Oro Wash near Rancho del Lago at approximately 7 AM on January 22nd.  A swift water rescue was performed to rescue the stranded motorist.

Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter estimated a wind gust of 94 mph near Ajo at 5:20 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong thunderstorms produced damaging microburst winds over portions of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix area, on August 24th. Strong winds caused damage to buildings, trees, and power poles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported numerous power poles downed in the town of Coolidge. Thunderstorm downburst winds were estimated to be in excess of 60kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure center over California continued to push abundant moisture over Arizona producing widespread showers and thunderstorms.  A jet maximum rotating around the low and into western Arizona helped produce large-scale lift and enhanced up-slope across the Mogollon Rim. The vertical wind shear also provided an environment for enhanced thunderstorm development.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail on the road caused a roll over near mile post 130 in Interstate 40.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southerly flow between a high pressure center over northern Mexico and a low pressure center off the southern California Coast brought increasing moisture over northern Arizona.  This brought isolated diurnal thunderstorms to the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two women were struck by lightning while standing on the South Rim of the Grand Canyon east of the Visitors Center.  Both women were taken to medical facilities, treated, and released.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms rolled through the area and at least one strong downburst was reported to have caused damage to homes and took down power lines.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large tree split and fell near a car at the Bashas parking lot near IndianSchool and Hayden in South Scottsdale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms generated damaging winds across portions of La Paz county on the afternoon of the 17th. Heavy rain fell as well which led to flooding of area washes such as Centennial wash near Wenden.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement officials reported that several power poles were blown down in the city of Wenden. The poles were downed due to strong thunderstorm winds estimated to be in excess of 50kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms generated damaging winds across portions of La Paz county on the afternoon of the 17th. Heavy rain fell as well which led to flooding of area washes such as Centennial wash near Wenden.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained weather spotter reported several power poles downed about 5 miles southeast of Bouse. The poles were downed due to strong thunderstorm outflow winds, estimated to be in excess of 50kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms generated damaging winds across portions of La Paz county on the afternoon of the 17th. Heavy rain fell as well which led to flooding of area washes such as Centennial wash near Wenden.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: County officials reported that the Centennial and Salome washes were running very heavy between the towns of Wenden and Salome. The strong flow was a result of heavy rain generated from strong thunderstorms in eastern La Paz county.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms rolled through the area and at least one strong downburst was reported to have caused damage to homes and took down power lines.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator in northeast Mesa reported an eight inch diameter tree blown down near Power and McDowell roads.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of thunderstorms rolled through the area and at least one strong downburst was reported to have caused damage to homes and took down power lines.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator reported power poles blown down near University Avenue and Signal Butte road in western Apache Junction. Microburst wind gusts were estimated to be approximately 60mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms generated locally heavy rainfall across portions of south central Arizona on August 24th. Rainfall was sufficient to cause some flooding of streets and washes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported very heavy rainfall near Dynamite and Cave Creek Roads, near the Cave Buttes Recreational Area. Rainfall was measured at 1.13 inches which fell in less than one hour; the heavy rain caused some street flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms generated locally heavy rainfall across portions of south central Arizona on August 24th. Rainfall was sufficient to cause some flooding of streets and washes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in Mesa reported very heavy rainfall, with a rainfall rate nearly four inches per hour. The heavy rain produced street flooding, with water flowing over the curb.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced heavy rain and damaging winds across portions of La Paz county, including the Alamo Lake Recreational Area, on the 24th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A park ranger at the Alamo Lake State Park reported downburst winds of at least 60 mph. The strong winds resulted in many tree limbs downed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms with damaging winds affected portions of southern Gila county on the 29th. Locally heavy rainfall also occurred with these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm microburst winds, estimated to be in excess of 60 knots, tore down the marquee sign from a pizza shop in the town of Claypool.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported that power poles were blown down at the junction of the Casa Grande Highway, and north White and Parker Road. Winds were estimated to be in excess of 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms across south-central Arizona produced damaging winds, hail and extremely heavy rainfall. Heavy rain with near zero visibility led to a massive traffic pileup in downtown Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator reported six inch diameter trees blown down in south central Maricopa. A trained spotter also reported a couple tree limbs blown down near the town of Maricopa. Winds were estimated in excess of 50 kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the higher terrain and propagated toward the metro Phoenix area late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter in the town of Chandler measured 1.30 inches of rainfall, most of which fell in a 30 minute period. Similar amounts of rain were reported by other spotters in Chandler and the east valley. The rainfall was sufficient to cause local flooding of streets, washes and low lying areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the higher terrain and propagated toward the metro Phoenix area late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm produced a wind gust to 61 mph, as reported by a trained weather spotter in Apache Junction. No damage was reported in association with the strong wind.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong thunderstorms produced damaging microburst winds over portions of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix area, on August 24th. Strong winds caused damage to buildings, trees, and power poles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damaging thunderstorm microburst winds, estimated to be in excess of 70kt, caused damage to portions of the Mayo Clinic Hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong thunderstorms produced damaging microburst winds over portions of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix area, on August 24th. Strong winds caused damage to buildings, trees, and power poles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms over Scottsdale generated microburst winds which toppled several power poles. Winds were estimated in excess of 50kt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A series of strong thunderstorms produced damaging microburst winds over portions of south central Arizona, including the greater Phoenix area, on August 24th. Strong winds caused damage to buildings, trees, and power poles.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported several power poles downed about 14 miles west of Casa Grande. The strong thunderstorm microburst winds that downed the poles also generated dense blowing dust, lowering visibility to one quarter mile.

Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Graham County law enforcement performed two swift water rescues just south of Safford during the late evening hours of January 21st.  Deputies responded at 10:15 PM to a truck caught in a wash on Chastain Lane, just north of Highway 366.  Law enforcement rescued the motorist of the truck.  There were no injuries, but the truck was fully engulfed by the water.  At approximately the same time, law enforcement spotted a van stuck in Stockton Wash, along Stockton Road south of Roper Lake.  The occupants of the van exited the vehicle and waded to safety, before the van was washed further down Stockton Wash.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Streets and highways were closed, homes and businesses were flooded after the third storm system of the week moved across the deserts and into the foothills. Some locations reported flooding during the day of January 21, while the major flooding in Wenden struck in the early morning hours of Friday, January 22.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Streets were closed near the Salt River as well as many creeks and washes after the third major storm system moved through the area. Rainfall prior to and during the day on Friday amounted to between 5 and 7 inches. The swollen creeks and washes left many low-lying areas flooded for days, with damaged homes and businesses.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Streets and highways were closed, homes and businesses were flooded after the third storm system of the week moved across the deserts and into the foothills. Some locations reported flooding during the day of January 21, while the major flooding in Wenden struck in the early morning hours of Friday, January 22.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 18 inches of water flooded roads and homes near Blackwater and Toki. In Arizona City, 4 homes had flood damage that lasted several days. Generally between 2 and 4 inches of rain fell in this area during the 5 days ending on January 22.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low moved across Southeast Arizona resulting in some mountain snow above 5000 feet.
Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow occurred across Northern Greenlee County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow occurred at elevations above 5000 feet in Graham County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five inches of snow occurred near Sonoita.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of snow accumulated on top of Kitt Peak.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow accumulated to a depth of 3.5 in the town of Oracle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A southwest flow with a relatively shallow moist layer moved over northern Arizona for several days before a cold front swept across the state.  The moist layer was below freezing however,  the layer was not thick enough or cold enough to allow microphysical dendritical growth to occur.  This caused periods of mist at the lower elevations and heavy accumulations of rime ice on trees and radio equipment above about 8500 feet. A cold front ushered in a much thicker layer of moisture, lift, and stronger winds. This brought accumulating snow.  Hundreds of trees were brought down by the weight of the ice, snow, and winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several days of low clouds, freezing temperatures, and steady winds produced large accumulations of rime ice on trees and radio towers above 8500 feet in the mountains around Flagstaff. Several hundred trees toppled at elevations of 9000 feet in the Kachina Peaks outside of Flagstaff.  Radio equipment atop Mt Elden near Flagstaff also sustained damage. Several radio stations were knocked off the air including the NWR. There were 49 traffic accidents due to snowy and ice roads.  There were 13 minor injuries. Eight inches of snow fell in Bellmont and 12 miles north of Parks and 6 inches fell at the Flagstaff Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and extremely cold Pacific storm system moved across Arizona December 29th and 30th. Heavy snow and strong winds produced hazardous weather conditions across much of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across the mountains of Yavapai County December 29th through the 30th.  Total snowfall amounts include: Crown King 17, 5 miles northwest of Prescott 11.5,  1 mile west of Prescott 9, and 2 miles northwest of Prescott 8.  I-40 near Ash Fork had delays and was closed periodically during the storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and extremely cold Pacific storm system moved across Arizona December 29th and 30th. Heavy snow and strong winds produced hazardous weather conditions across much of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across northern Gila December 29th through the 30th.  Total snowfall amounts include: Pine and Christopher Creek both had 16, 18 miles east of Payson 12,  Star Valley 9, and Payson 6.5-7.3.  State Route 87 was closed just south of Payson while a multiple car collision block both north and south lanes of traffic.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and extremely cold Pacific storm system moved across Arizona December 29th and 30th. Heavy snow and strong winds produced hazardous weather conditions across much of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across the Kaibab Plateau with 12 inches reported at Jacob Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow accumulated at the lower elevations of the Santa Catalina Mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Another winter storm moved across southeast Arizona during the nighttime hours of January 22nd into 23rd. This storm resulted in additional rainfall and low elevation snowfall.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow occurred in numerous low elevation areas of Cochise County including Tombstone where 2 inches accumulated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers developed and became moderate to heavy at times. Phoenix set a record precipitation amount on Wednesday December 29th. A line of thunderstorms with locally strong winds also developed and moved eastward during the early morning hours of the 30th. Phoenix Sky Harbor airport had a wind gust of 49 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A billboard along State Highway 188 across from Spring Creek Store was damaged and a nearby tree was uprooted. In addition, part of a rooftop cooler was blown down.  Finally, a member of the public reported a measured wind gust to 59 mph at 435 am in Tonto Village. This person also indicated that 1.84 inches of storm total precipitation was observed.

A line of thunderstorms with locally strong winds also developed and moved eastward during the early morning hours of the 30th. Phoenix Sky Harbor airport had a wind gust of 49 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of light snow and graupel (soft hail) across the greater Phoenix area including Scottsdale, Paradise Valley, Mesa, and Apache Junction.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across the eastern Mogollon Rim.  Snowfall totals include (inches): Forest Lakes 30, Clints Well 18, Heber Overgaard 12, and Clay Springs 10.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong and gusty straight-line winds, estimated to be around 45 mph, caused several tree branches to be blown down 5 miles southwest of Paradise Valley.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damaging thunderstorm winds downed power lines which fell on top of several mobile homes on North Shannon Road in northwest Tucson.  A total of six houses were evacuated.  A small fire started in one mobile home, burning the attic and causing smoke damage to the interior of the house.  A tree approximately one foot in diameter was downed by thunderstorm winds near the intersection of Oracle Road and Wilds Road.

Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times. EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damaging thunderstorm winds downed the main transmission power line on the Tohono Oodham Nation, at approximately 9 PM.  A major power failure resulted, affecting over 15,000 people.  Mesonet at Sasabe measured a thunderstorm wind gust of 58 mph at 9:33 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of flooding in Southern Gila County. Residents were evacuated along the wash south of San Carlos. Residents near Tonto Basin were stranded for several days after flows of about 60,000 cfs were measured along the Tonto Creek. By Monday, January 25th, the flow was down to about 550 cfs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two power poles were blown down just south of the intersection of Gilbert Road and Hunt highway.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An exceptionally strong Pacific storm system impacted southeast Arizona, producing a wide variety of weather.  Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times.  Also, several severe thunderstorms produced wind damage during the evening of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds resulted in many reports of damage across eastern Pima County.  Mesonet at Summerhaven on Mount Lemmon measured a wind gust of 75 mph at 1:37 PM.  A power pole was downed near the intersection of Wetmore Road and La Cholla Road in Tucson.  Damaging winds tore the roof off a house at Three Points around 3 PM, with the roof landing on a nearby truck. Near Green Valley, several traffic signals were damaged at Duval Mine and La Canada Roads, and at Calle Verde and Nogales Highway.  More traffic signals were damaged by the high winds in Sahuarita, on Sahuarita Boulevard.  High winds downed several power lines in Oro Valley, and several trees in Sahuarita during the late afternoon hours.  Just southeast of Sahuarita, the winds caused partial roof damage to a pecan processing plant.   |A large tree was downed, falling into a house near 9500 East Harrison Circle in east Tucson at approximately 5 PM.  High winds damaged a parking awning at an apartment complex at Ina and Mona Lisa in Tucson.  Another tree was downed by the winds near Congress and Grande.  Throughout the evening hours, numerous trees and power lines were downed on Mount Lemmon near Summerhaven.  Frequent wind gusts near 70 mph on Mount Lemmon resulted in a complete power outage on the mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An exceptionally strong Pacific storm system impacted southeast Arizona, producing a wide variety of weather.  Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times.  Also, several severe thunderstorms produced wind damage during the evening of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several reports of high winds and wind damage were received from Cochise County during the afternoon and evening hours. The ASOS at Fort Huachuca measured a wind gust of 63 mph at 1:55 PM MST.  At approximately 6 PM, a 70-foot transmission power pole was downed by the high winds along Buffalo Soldier Trail near Sierra Vista, resulting in a loss of power to approximately half of Sierra Vista.  Seven additional power poles were downed near the intersection of Ramsey Road and Highway 92, resulting in power losses to another 500 residences.  A trained spotter measured a wind gust of 67 mph at Bisbee at 10:39 PM MST, while another trained spotter measured a wind gust of 78 mph, 6 miles northwest of Pearce-Sunsites, at 11:30 PM MST.  Damaging winds downed several trees across Monument Roadway near Chiricahua National Monument.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Considerable moisture and rapid overnight cooling resulted in dense fog.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several spotters reported dense fog throughout metropolitan Phoenix. Two reports indicated zero visibility in the far southeast area of Queen Creek. The widespread dense fog occurred during the Friday morning rush hour and was a significant hazard to area motorists, even though no major accidents were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moving across northern Arizona brought numerous snow showers, gusty winds, and reduced visibility to the high country.  The snow level was above 6500 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rain and snow started falling over the high country of the Western Mogollon Rim late in the evening on November 20th. Snow on roadways began impacting travel by daylight on November 21st. There were numerous vehicles that slid off roads and several vehicles rolled over because of the slippery conditions. The Flagstaff Airport received about 3 inches of snow.

Widespread heavy valley rain and higher elevation snow affected all of southeast Arizona.  Additionally, the storm produced a significant wind event, with strong and damaging winds reported at many locations, along with areas of blowing dust. Heavy rainfall and melting snowpack produced some flooding of area streams and washes, and the combination of heavy snow and strong winds produced blizzard conditions on the highest elevations at times.  Also, several severe thunderstorms produced wind damage during the evening of the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds caused significant roof damage to several structures in the Tohono Oodham Nation.  One residence roof was damaged in Sells, one in Gu Vo, and three more in Hickiwan.  The residents of the damaged homes were displaced into shelters.  In addition, hundreds of saguaro cacti across the Tohono Oodham Nation were blown over by the damaging winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure moved across Southeast Arizona during the afternoon of March 7th and into March 8th. This produced valley rain and mountain snow above 5000 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was about 14 of snow on top of Mt. Graham.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of large trees blown down in Yuma.

Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sheriff reported a road closure due to flooding at Cotton road and Camelback. Numerous other streets in the West Valley had some flooding later in the day.

Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Four semi trucks overturned on Interstate Highway 8 at milepost 42 and milepost 67 due to high winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second in a series of strong Pacific storms moved across northern Arizona with widespread heavy precipitation.  The snow level dropped down to  between 5000 and 5500 feet elevation by the storm moved east.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall spread across the White Mountains overnight and into the morning hours.   Some of the snowfall reports include:  Lakeside 8 to 12 inches,  Sunrise Mountain 10 inches and Alpine 9 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second in a series of strong Pacific storms moved across northern Arizona with widespread heavy precipitation.  The snow level dropped down to  between 5000 and 5500 feet elevation by the storm moved east.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow and strong winds were felt across northern Gila County.  The snow level remained above 5000 feet.  Tonto Village received 6 inches of snow. Power went out in the town of Pine.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second in a series of strong Pacific storms moved across northern Arizona with widespread heavy precipitation.  The snow level dropped down to  between 5000 and 5500 feet elevation by the storm moved east.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell along the Eastern Mogollon Rim.  Snowfall totals for this one storm include:  Clints Well 16 inches,  Heber 13 inches,  Clay Springs 14 to 15 inches, and Forest Lakes 16 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ten utility poles along US 60 near Gladden were blown down by high winds. Power was cut off to Salome, Wenden and other communities until 2:15 pm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five people were rescued from flood waters at 389th and Orangewood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and extremely cold Pacific storm system moved across Arizona December 29th and 30th. Heavy snow and strong winds produced hazardous weather conditions across much of northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow, strong winds, and blowing snow moved across the western Mogollon Rim from early morning on December 29th through much of the day on the 30th. Some snowfall totals include: 4 miles south of Williams 30 inches, 12 miles north of Parks 24 inches, Munds Park 19.5 inches, Bellmont 18.8 inches,  Williams 18 inches, Flagstaff Airport 17.2 inches, Kachina Village 16 inches, and 15 inches in north Fort Valley. Over 150 motorist needed emergency assistance in the Flagstaff area with roll overs, slide offs, and wrecks.  Both I-17 and Highway 89 south of Flagstaff were closed due to the dangerous conditions.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous power poles blown down due to thunderstorm winds and heavy rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. EVENT_NARRATIVE: APS power lines were down from flood waters in the Salt River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain and some small hail were reported across many desert areas. One of the heaviest rainfall areas was in Yuma,  where .89 inch set a new record rainfall for the 7th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Small hail reported in the Yuma area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure moved across Southeast Arizona February 28th resulting in valley rain and higher elevation snowfall above 4500 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The town of Oracle had two inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An area of low pressure moved across Southeast Arizona February 28th resulting in valley rain and higher elevation snowfall above 4500 feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several inches of snow occurred in the lower elevations of the Santa Catalina Mountains above 4500 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain and some small hail were reported across many desert areas. One of the heaviest rainfall areas was in Yuma,  where .89 inch set a new record rainfall for the 7th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several reports of hail across the metro area. Hail size was 1/4 to 1/2 inch. Brief heavy rain and gusty winds were also reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds from thunderstorms moved through the eastern portions of the area. Wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph were common in Phoenix. The strongest wind at Deer Valley airport was 45 mph, which was at 1:51 PM.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Small hail and wind gusts to 40 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very strong jet stream moving across the Pacific brought abundant moisture and significant precipitation to northern Arizona on January 21th.  The snow level started out around 3400 feet with  significant snow above 5000 feet.  The jet stream buckled during the day on the 21st and brought much warmer air to the area. This raised the snow level to almost 8000 feet for several hours.  A strong cold front then moved across the and brought the snow level crashing to near 4000 feet.  The cold front also brought high winds to the area. This storm caused power outages, flooding, evacuations, mudslides, collapsed roofs, and wind damage.||The higher elevations received between 4 and 8 feet of snow from all three storms.  Lower elevations along the south slopes of the Mogollon Rim and mountains of Gila and Yavapai County received up to 12 inches of rain from all three storms.  Snowfall and rainfall records were shattered.  The combination of heavy rain and melting snow caused flooding problems in the lower elevations.  Deep snow and mud stranded many people and livestock across the Hopi and Navajo Nations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A family of four partially crossed the bed of the Agua Fria River in a truck (SUV) when flash flooding occurred.  They became stuck in the flood waters. A swift water rescue was in possible and a helicopter could not be dispatched due to the weather conditions. The 6 year old boy was subsequently swept downstream and drown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm hit northern Arizona with widespread snow and rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flagstaff area received between 8 and 11 inches of new snow. Kachina Village received 11.9 inches of new snow.  A spotter 13 miles northwest of Flagstaff received 13-15 inches of new snow.  Munds Park received 8 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm hit northern Arizona with widespread snow and rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pinetop-Lakeside received between 8 and 9 inches of new snow. White River (5300 feet elevation) received 5 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm hit northern Arizona with widespread snow and rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Clints Well received between 9 and 10 inches of new snow.  The Heber-Overgaard area received between 8 and 10 inches of new snow. Forest Lakes received 18 inches of new snow. Cebecue (5000 feet elevation) received 5 inches of new snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A Pacific Storm brought heavy snow to the Mogollon Rim and mountains of northeastern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell across the Western Mogollon Rim.  Snowfall reports include: Mormon Lake 14 inches, Flagstaff Airport 12 inches, Munds Park and Bellemont 11 inches, and Williams 10-12 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and isolated thunderstorms were associated with a low pressure system and cold front that spread across much of south central Arizona. Winds gusted to 47 mph at Scottsdale airport and 40 mph at Sky Harbor airport around midnight.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm and dime size hail reported by spotter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Showers and isolated thunderstorms were associated with a low pressure system and cold front that spread across much of south central Arizona. Winds gusted to 47 mph at Scottsdale airport and 40 mph at Sky Harbor airport around midnight.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain and small hail reported. Hail diameter was about one quarter inch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold winter storm moved across northern Arizona with heavy snow.  The heavier amounts of new snow were from half a foot to a foot and a half.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Jacob Lake received up to 30 inches of new snow with a snow depth of 42 inches. Sixteen inches of snowfall was reported one mile south of the Grand Canyon National Park entrance on the North Rim.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Six inches of snow fell at 6700 feet 6 miles south of Prescott.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front approaching Arizona brought very strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.  The strong winds also caused large areas of blowing dust and sand.  Roads were closed due to low visibility in the blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dust reduced the visibility to less than 1/2 mile in many locations.  The visibility between Luepp and Bird Springs on Indian Route was near zero. The wind was estimated to be gusting between 60 and 70 MPH.  A 17 mile section Interstate 40 was closed between Two Guns and Winslow for 5 hours. There was a 6 vehicle pile up on I-40 near the westbound Leupp exit. Five people were taken to the hospital.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front brought strong winds to northern Arizona. The strong winds created blowing dust which closed roads again.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The dust reduced the visibility enough by 900AM that Highway Patrol had to escort vehicles on the I-40 near Winslow. The interstate was closed from 1230 PM to 530 PM due to restricted visibility in blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front approaching Arizona brought very strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.  The strong winds also caused large areas of blowing dust and sand.  Roads were closed due to low visibility in the blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Show Low airport ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 59 MPH.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure center moving across the western United States produced strong winds and significant blowing dust across northern Arizona. The winds caused power outages and the dust caused road closures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Blowing dust reduced the visibility down to 10 feet at times across the Little Colorado River Valley at times from late morning through late evening. Interstate 40 was close for much of the time.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN NAVAJO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure center moving across the western United States produced strong winds and significant blowing dust across northern Arizona. The winds caused power outages and the dust caused road closures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The wind at the Winslow Airport gusted over 58 MPH close to 11 hours. There were power outages and power poles were blown down in Winslow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure center moving across the western United States produced strong winds and significant blowing dust across northern Arizona. The winds caused power outages and the dust caused road closures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The wind gusts were estimated at 55 to 60 MPH across at Canyon de Chelly.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A low pressure center moving across the western United States produced strong winds and significant blowing dust across northern Arizona. The winds caused power outages and the dust caused road closures.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ADOT anemometer at Two Guns measured a peak wind gust of 73 MPH.Tuba City had wind gusts to 60 MPH.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An intense storm system passed through the Four Corners region on May 2.  An increase in pressure gradients resulted in strong winds and areas of blowing dust across southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fatal three car collision occurred on Interstate 10 in Pinal County near mile marker 214 and Picacho State Park.  At approximately 12:45 PM MST, a semi-truck slowed down on the interstate due to limited visibility in blowing dust.  A car following the semi-truck failed to slow down, and lost control as it attempted to avoid a collision with the semi.  The car collided with a wall and the semi, before being struck by a third car.  The driver of the second car died on scene.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep low pressure system brought widespread winds of 30 to 40 mph. Some damage was reported as gusts exceeded 50 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds were reported at the time that a boat went down on Roosevelt Lake. One person slightly injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Gusty winds from thunderstorms moved through the eastern portions of the area. Wind gusts of 30 to 40 mph were common in Phoenix. The strongest wind at Deer Valley airport was 45 mph, which was at 1:51 PM.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least one large tree was blown down as a line of thunderstorms rolled through the far East Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold storm system passed through northern Arizona and the Great Basin, resulting in strong southwest winds across much of southeast Arizona.  The strongest winds were focused well ahead of the system's cold front, across Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Fort Huachuca ASOS measured a wind gust of 59 mph.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Winslow ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 66 MPH.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Window Rock ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 59 MPH.  The ADOT anemometer at Lupton reported a peak wind gust of 58 MPH.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Near zero visibility in blowing dust reported on Indian Route 15 between Leupp and Bird Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front approaching Arizona brought very strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.  The strong winds also caused large areas of blowing dust and sand.  Roads were closed due to low visibility in the blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Flagstaff ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 61 MPH.  A RAWS in Doney Park reported a peak wind gust of 59 MPH.  A camper and box trailers that were being towed east bound on I-40 near Flagstaff blew over.  Around 4,500 customers were without power in the Flagstaff area for an hour or less when trees fell into power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep low pressure system brought widespread winds of 30 to 40 mph. Some damage was reported as gusts exceeded 50 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gust o 58 mph reported at Goodyear airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep low pressure system brought widespread winds of 30 to 40 mph. Some damage was reported as gusts exceeded 50 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large palo verde tree blown over in Ahwatukee.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep low pressure system brought widespread winds of 30 to 40 mph. Some damage was reported as gusts exceeded 50 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds damaged power poles and lines near the top of South Mountain, near 39th Avenue and Dunlop, and near Central and Dobbins. Power outages affected about 1000 customers at the peak of the strongest winds.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front approaching Arizona brought very strong winds to the Little Colorado River Valley.  The strong winds also caused large areas of blowing dust and sand.  Roads were closed due to low visibility in the blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ADOT anemometer at Two Guns measured a peak wind gust of 70 MPH. Several barrels blew off a truck on State Route 160 near Tuba City and disabled a following semi truck.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep low pressure system brought widespread winds of 30 to 40 mph. Some damage was reported as gusts exceeded 50 mph.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes in Chandler, near Kyrene and Ray, had roof tiles blown off. Tree limbs were also blown down.  One woman described the wind sounding like a roaring train.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Streets and highways were closed, homes and businesses were flooded after the third storm system of the week moved across the deserts and into the foothills. Some locations reported flooding during the day of January 21, while the major flooding in Wenden struck in the early morning hours of Friday, January 22.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flood waters mainly from the Centennial Wash moved through Wenden in the early morning hours. Law enforcement began evacuating people before waters closed the local streets and roads. About 200 people took shelter at a local high school. Flood waters up to 6 feet deep took out power poles, telephone poles, trees, damaged roads and homes. The entire town of Wenden was underwater for several days.  Power was restored in most areas within 2 weeks. Generally, about 3 to 5 inches of rain fell in the watershed area during the week of January 18-22.

Deep snow and mud stranded many people and livestock across the Hopi and Navajo Nations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between 4 and 8 inches of snow fell below 5000 feet in Yavapai Country.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between 28 and 38 inches of snow fell across the Flagstaff area.  The snow turned to rain for between 4 and 6 hours when about 3/4 of an inch of rain fell. EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread rain, heavy at times, resulted in numerous flooded streets, and low spots. Strong winds associated with a line of thunderstorms caused considerable damage to property and some minor injuries. Phoenix established a new all-time record low pressure of 29.20 inches on the 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three large trees blown over at Bell Road and 16th Street. A large tent at the Russo Steele Auction in Scottsdale near Mayo Blvd and Scottsdale Rd was destroyed and blown into nearby State Highway Loop 101 when winds collapsed the tent onto many classic cars. Small damage at a nearby Barrett Jackson Auction. Three minor injuries reported.
