EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable atmosphere resulted in scattered thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona, beginning relatively early during the midday hours.  With the large amounts of moisture available, the primary threats from thunderstorms were heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced torrential rainfall on the north side of the Catalina Mountains, producing flash flooding.  Rainfall estimates were locally as high as six inches in portions of southeast Pinal County, specifically near Camp Bonito. Law enforcement barricaded Mt. Lemmon and Peppersauce Roads due to flash flooding.  A trained spotter measured 2.40 inches of rain 4 miles southeast of Oracle.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable atmosphere resulted in scattered thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona, beginning relatively early during the midday hours.  With the large amounts of moisture available, the primary threats from thunderstorms were heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Torrential rainfall in the Huachuca Mountains led to flash flooding at the Ramsey Canyon Preserve.  USGS rain gauges measured 3.82 inches of rainfall, leading to rapid rises in Ramsey Creek.  Ramsey Canyon Road was closed at Calle Metate due to flooding and debris.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong weather disturbance moving across northern Arizona produced areas of strong winds and blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter in the community of Many Farms reported less than 1/4 mile visibility in blowing dust.  Department of Public Safety in Kayenta reported blowing dust but,  with good visibility and no travel issues.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered in northeast Arizona on July 17th, with a favorable easterly or southeasterly steering flow across southeast Arizona.  Plentiful moisture resulted in ample instability, and several scattered thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported windows blown out of a house in Sells.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered in northeast Arizona on July 17th, with a favorable easterly or southeasterly steering flow across southeast Arizona.  Plentiful moisture resulted in ample instability, and several scattered thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms resulted in tree limbs eight inches in diameter downed near Elgin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered over central Arizona on July 19th. Numerous thunderstorms rotated around this upper high, and affected much of southeast Arizona during the afternoon and evening hours.  Afternoon and early evening storms impacted mainly Cochise and Santa Cruz counties, with late evening and early morning storms impacting the Tucson metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A lightning strike caused a house fire during the early morning hours of July 20th.  The lightning strike likely occurred around 1 AM as storms impacted north Tucson, with the sparks smoldering in the roof's insulation until a neighbor noticed the fire spreading around 5 AM. The fire resulted in extension damage to the home before the fire was extinguished.

CZ_NAME: LITTLE COLORADO RIVER VALLEY IN COCONINO COUNTY
EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across Arizona with very strong surface winds and blowing dust.  The highest wind speed,  77 MPH,  topped hurricane force and the visibility dropped to below 15 feet in places.  Interstate 40 was closed for nine hours and at least 3 vehicles blew over.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two RV trailers and a semi trailer were blown over on I-40 near Two Guns.  The ADOT anemometer near Two Guns had a peak wind gust of 77 MPH at 832 AM MST.

Numerous thunderstorms rotated around this upper high, and affected much of southeast Arizona during the afternoon and evening hours.  Afternoon and early evening storms impacted mainly Cochise and Santa Cruz counties, with late evening and early morning storms impacting the Tucson metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck palm tree in the front yard of a home on Abrego Road, which spread to other nearby trees.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported wind damage to awnings of a residence near Dragoon. The Willcox sheriff reported a horse was killed near Cochise, after a downed power line electrocuted the animal.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered near the Four Corners region on the evening of July 20th, with southeast steering flow over southeast Arizona. Thunderstorm complexes that originated in Sonora propagated northwestward into Cochise County, while other storms arrived from New Mexico and affected Greenlee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: ASOS at the Bisbee-Douglas Airport measured a thunderstorm wind gust of 58 mph at 731 PM MST.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported Ward Canyon Road was closed due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered in northeast Arizona on July 17th, with a favorable easterly or southeasterly steering flow across southeast Arizona.  Plentiful moisture resulted in ample instability, and several scattered thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds caused damage near the village of Sheldon in Greenlee County. Several trees were uprooted, and a backyard trampoline was blown 100 yards by the thunderstorm winds.  Doors of mobile homes were blown inward.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in Sierra Vista, and caused a fire near the kitchen stove.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home, causing a fire in the attic.  The home was unoccupied at the time, and there were no injuries.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Willcox sheriff reported that thunderstorm winds blew down an outbuilding in Bowie.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 24 miles SSW of Colorado City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful storm system brought widespread high winds to the Mojave Desert and southern Great Basin.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This gust occurred 9 miles WNW of Tuweep.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably cold Pacific storm system entered Arizona on April 29th.  As the storm and its associated cold front entered southeast Arizona, strong winds were focused well ahead of the cold front across Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at the Bisbee-Douglas Airport measured a wind gust of 60 mph at 12:31 PM MST.  The ADOT weather station at San Simon on Interstate 10 measured a wind gust of 60 mph. Several trained spotters in Cochise County reported wind gusts near  60 mph during the late morning and afternoon hours, with one trained spotter in Hereford reporting minor shingle roof damage at about 1:30 PM.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere resulted in numerous thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona on July 27th.  Storms initiated relatively early during the midday hours, with the primary threats being heavy flooding rain and damaging winds during the afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads, including Sahuarita Road and Old Nogales Highway, were closed due to flooding caused by heavy rain. Franco Wash flowed across Summit Road in two spots, also resulting in its closure.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms producing a large number of lightning strikes sparked several wildfires in the mountains of northern Greenlee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The lightning-caused Paradise fire burned over 6300 acres of the Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest east of Hannagan Meadow. While no structures were threatened, a 10 mile stretch of highway 191 was closed for several days due to smoke.  An incident meteorologist from NWS Tucson was assigned to the fire for nearly 2 weeks. The fire was turned over to a local Type 3 team on the morning of June 29th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An extremely moist southeast flow across Arizona brought widespread showers, thunderstorms,  heavy rain, and flash flooding to northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding in Antelope Canyon. People were stranded on ledges in this slot canyon when a four and a half foot wall of water came rushing through the canyon.  Five members of a 12 member Swiss tourist group were injured. A woman and her 9 year old son sustained scrapes and busies as they were washed downstream. Three men were also injured.  A second wall of water came rushing through the canyon after the people were rescued.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist atmosphere resulted in numerous thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona on July 27th.  Storms initiated relatively early during the midday hours, with the primary threats being heavy flooding rain and damaging winds during the afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm produced a well-developed funnel cloud near Sonoita.  Video of the funnel cloud was obtained through the broadcast media.  There was no direct evidence from the video that the funnel made contact with the ground.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Eagle Rock Fire was started by lightning on June 11,  2010 at about 6:00 PM.  It ended up burning 3,374 acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Eagle Rock Fire was started by lightning late afternoon on Friday June 11th near Eagle Rock on the east side of Sitgreaves Mountain on the Kaibab National Forest.  The fire made very little movement and the fire went undetected until Red Flag conditions were experienced on the fire June 15th.  Fifty residences in the area were evacuated when the winds fanned the fire. No homes were lost in this fire.  However, there were two out buildings that were destroyed. As many as 664 people were assigned to the fire during the peak activity.  Fire fighting efforts cost close to 4 million dollars.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms formed within a very moist atmosphere over southeast Arizona, producing heavy flooding rainfall at many locales.  Many creeks, washes and rivers experienced heavy runoff and elevated flows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Santa Cruz River near Trico Road was reported to be topping its banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms formed within a very moist atmosphere over southeast Arizona, producing heavy flooding rainfall at many locales.  Many creeks, washes and rivers experienced heavy runoff and elevated flows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Santa Cruz river at Cortaro Rd rose to about 3 feet above its flood stage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered over central Arizona on July 19th. Numerous thunderstorms rotated around this upper high, and affected much of southeast Arizona during the afternoon and evening hours.  Afternoon and early evening storms impacted mainly Cochise and Santa Cruz counties, with late evening and early morning storms impacting the Tucson metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The ASOS at the Bisbee-Douglas Airport measured a wind gust of 68 mph at 556 pm MST. Law enforcement reported that thunderstorm winds blew a roof off a residence on Double Adobe Road, approximately 7 miles west of the Bisbee-Douglas Airport.  The Bisbee sheriff reported thunderstorm winds blew a roof completely off a large barn along Burnt Adobe Road, and snapped four power poles. A tree was uprooted and fell on a building at the Douglas Municipal Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local broadcast media reported up to 3 dozen trees damaged or uprooted in Rancho Vistoso neighborhood.  A few ceramic roof tiles were also blown off homes.  In addition, a NWS Employee reported several trees down in Dove Mountain with one tree leaning up against a home.  There was only slight tile damage to the home.  Also, local broadcast media reported trees and power lines down in Marana at Interstate 10 and Marana/Trico Road as well as a roof ripped off a mobile home. The Marana Airport also sustained damage.  Two small airplanes were ripped from their tie down chains and were flipped over while another plane was blown into a field. A large hangar door was blown off its tracks and a few other hangars also sustained light damage.  In the same area, several power poles and lines were downed on Twin Peaks Road east of N. Sandario Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed and moved across southern portions of Tucson producing 50 mph winds at the Tucson International Airport, but no damage was reported.  Storms moved to the northwest through western portions of Tucson with an outflow racing out in advance.  Damage occurred to six saguaro cacti, one which was over 35 feet tall, at El Camino De Cerro and the Tucson Mountains.  Tucson weather observation site CW8334, which is 5 miles southeast of the damage, recorded a wind gust of 42 mph at 5:09 pm.  Based on damage and stronger velocities where the damage was reported, winds were estimated to be about 50 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An owner of a grape vineyard in Elgin reported hail for 15 minutes with the largest being golf ball size (1.75).  Damage to the vineyard was extensive, with nearly all the 2010 grape crop lost.  Crop losses at this one vineyard were estimated at six tons which translated to between 10 and 15 thousand dollars, ultimately resulting in a loss of wine valued at 100 to 150 thousand dollars.  Extensive damage was also sustained at other vineyards in area.  Hail also dented vehicles and broke their windows.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An isolated severe thunderstorm produced a downburst in the vicinity of Snyder Road and Houghton Road downing several power poles and lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law Enforcement reported Brewery Gulch and several streets flooded in Old Town Bisbee and Banning Creek northwest of Bisbee also flooded.  In addition, flooding damaged fiber-optic lines near Tombstone.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist and unstable atmosphere resulted in scattered thunderstorms developing across southeast Arizona, beginning relatively early during the midday hours.  With the large amounts of moisture available, the primary threats from thunderstorms were heavy rain and flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A pecan grove worker was struck by lightning while working near Green Valley. The man received non-life threatening injuries. A palm tree was also set ablaze by a lightning strike in the 700 block of West Rio San Pedro in Green Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms formed within a very moist atmosphere over southeast Arizona, producing heavy flooding rainfall at many locales.  Many creeks, washes and rivers experienced heavy runoff and elevated flows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding from rainfall south of the International Border resulting in the Nogales Wash rising 7 feet in 15 minutes. This caused some damage to the bank protection including three concrete panels in the wash.  Part of Old Tucson Road, which parallels the wash, was partially eroded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Numerous thunderstorms formed within a very moist atmosphere over southeast Arizona, producing heavy flooding rainfall at many locales.  Many creeks, washes and rivers experienced heavy runoff and elevated flows.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Torrential rainfall across portions of eastern Pima county resulted in numerous reports of flash flooding in the Tucson metro area.  Flash flooding was observed on Tanque Verde Creek.  The flow of the creek peeked at 11.69 feet at Tanque Verde Guest Ranch, resulting in the flooding of approximately 30 homes on Barbary Coast Road, Gold Dust Road, and Kitt Carson.  Additionally, numerous swift water rescues were performed in the Tucson metro area, near the county fairgrounds, in the Recon Valley area, and on the Old Spanish Trail in the Hilton Head Ranch area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Law enforcement reported penny size hail covering the ground at mule tunnel northwest of Bisbee.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Above average moisture fueled thunderstorm development across Southern Arizona. Thunderstorms produced locally heavy rainfall and gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 6 inch diameter tree was uprooted by microburst winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A return to favorable monsoon conditions, including abundant moisture and a disturbance, brought strong thunderstorms and heavy rain to southern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: This report goes along with thunderstorm which produced 1.75 inch diameter hail in Elgin.  Just before and during the storm, the vineyard owner reported an estimate of at least 60 mph winds.  Damage was limited to farm structures and not dwellings.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Above average moisture and a thunderstorm complex from Sonora, Mexico helped set up a flash flood event across southwest Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flood waters flowed across the right lane of Highway 90 north of Huachuca City.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot weather persisted through South Central Arizona during the last week of the month.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Maricopa County officials reported one heat caused death late in June. A 21 month old girl died after being left in a car in Phoenix. Temperatures in Phoenix were several degrees above average, and the highs reached 113 degrees on the 24th and the 30th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft remained anchored across northern Arizona on July 15th, with deep easterly flow across southeast Arizona. A disturbance rotating around the upper high in this easterly flow enhanced storm organization and intensity across central and western Pima county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused roof damage to six homes in Pisinimo, with two complete roof failures reported, and four roofs partially damaged.  Several power poles were damaged by the thunderstorm winds as well, with five communities reporting power outages.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered in northeast Arizona on July 17th, with a favorable easterly or southeasterly steering flow across southeast Arizona.  Plentiful moisture resulted in ample instability, and several scattered thunderstorms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorm winds caused damage across southern portions of Tucson.  A trained spotter reported several trees downed near Rita Ranch at 5 PM MST.  Power lines were reported down by the winds at two different locations near the Tucson International Airport.  Thunderstorm winds ripped the roof off a mobile home near the intersection of Interstate 19 and Valencia Road.  In addition, several other trees and power lines were downed by the thunderstorm winds across southwest portions of Tucson.  One of these trees was downed on a residence.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered over central Arizona on July 19th. Numerous thunderstorms rotated around this upper high, and affected much of southeast Arizona during the afternoon and evening hours.  Afternoon and early evening storms impacted mainly Cochise and Santa Cruz counties, with late evening and early morning storms impacting the Tucson metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm downburst winds caused wind damage across portions of the Tucson metro area. Several trees and power lines were downed near the intersection of Ina and Oracle Roads, and the winds caused some minor roof damage in this area as well.  Another power pole was downed near Ajo Way and 6th Avenue, and also near Glenn and Alvernon Way. Small trees were reported in the road near the intersection of Grant and Wilmot.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A baby girl died after being left in a car in Kingman, AZ.  The high temperature was 96 degrees, and the temperature inside the car was reported to be over 100 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A baby girl died after being left in a car in Kingman, AZ. The high temperature was 96 degrees, and the temperature inside the car was reported to be over 100 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably deep Pacific trough interacted with monsoon moisture to produce scattered severe thunderstorms and flash flooding across the Mojave Desert.

An upper level low pressure system was centered in central Arizona on July 24th.  Drier air rotated around this feature into southeast Arizona, limiting thunderstorm development.  Enough moisture was present to produce isolated short-lived thunderstorms that were capable of briefly damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported two chain link fence support poles were downed by thunderstorm winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Adequate moisture and southerly flow allowed for another day of thunderstorm activity.  Heavy rainfall, flash flooding, and large hail occurred under the strongest storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several sections of Moson Road were covered in flood waters and debris. Spotter reported a rainfall total of 2.30 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure aloft was centered over central Arizona on July 19th. Numerous thunderstorms rotated around this upper high, and affected much of southeast Arizona during the afternoon and evening hours.  Afternoon and early evening storms impacted mainly Cochise and Santa Cruz counties, with late evening and early morning storms impacting the Tucson metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm microburst produced damaging winds in Rio Rico.  A portion of a home's roof was blown off, and brought down a power line as it landed in Rio Rico Drive.  Thunderstorm winds also blew down several dying trees between the Santa Cruz River bridge and the railroad tracks, and blew down a cyprus tree onto the roof of a home on East Rio Rico Drive.  Winds were measured on a neighbor's anemometer at 75 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large storm system moving over the western United States and a strong cold front moving across northern Arizona brought high winds and blowing dust to much of the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter in Kykotsmovi Village reported visibility down to 10 feet at times in blowing dust. Blowing dust in Pinon dropped the visibility down to 200 yards at times.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The dispatch center in Chinle reported near zero visibility on Highway 191 north of Chinle.  Police in Kayenta reported blowing dust with visibility down to 100 feet.  The community of Many Farms had visibility down to 25 feet in blowing dust.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Arizona Department of Public Safety weather station at Two Guns reported a peak wind gust of 60 MPH.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel size hail began at 520 pm, eventually growing as large as 1.25 inches in diameter.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter in Kykotsmovi Village reported visibility down to 10 feet at times in blowing dust. The visibility dropped to 20 feet at times in Dilkon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain resulted in flooding of streets and low spots in parts of Northwest and North Central Pinal County. Showers and thunderstorms moved slowly across the area late on the 30th and continued in Arizona City on Saturday the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported 0.75 inch in 20 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain resulted in flooding of streets and low spots in parts of Northwest and North Central Pinal County. Showers and thunderstorms moved slowly across the area late on the 30th and continued in Arizona City on Saturday the 31st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Total rain during a 30 minute period was 0.51 inch.
